# t20.4.1 - Restore DB2 bootstrap and seed-data path for canonical login and quote flows

## Summary

Restored the preserved DB2 bootstrap path by keeping `/config` operator-gated by default while
allowing the legacy `action=buildDB` flow through only when the canonical seed dataset is absent.
That keeps the post-review auth boundary in place for normal config/admin traffic, but removes the
circular dependency on `uid:0` existing before the app can create `uid:0` and the seeded `s:*`
quotes on a fresh DB2 lane.

## Deliverables

- Anonymous bootstrap exception is now scoped to `/config?action=buildDB` only, and only while the
  canonical bootstrap data (`uid:0` and `s:1`) is missing.
- Focused MVC coverage now locks down three cases: anonymous `/config` stays unauthorized,
  anonymous `buildDB` is allowed before seeding, and anonymous `buildDB` is blocked again once the
  canonical data exists.
- Live DB2 validation showed the packaged Boot WAR can seed the lane, return the preserved welcome
  surface for `uid:0 / xxx`, and serve a populated `s:1` quote after bootstrap.

## Test Results

- Command: `cmd /d /c "set JAVA_HOME=%USERPROFILE%\scoop\apps\microsoft17-jdk\current&& set PATH=%JAVA_HOME%\bin;%PATH%&& mvn -Dtest=TradeConfigControllerTest test"`
- Command: `pwsh -NoLogo -NoProfile -File .\scripts\start-db2-primary-lane.ps1 -ResetData`
- Command: `cmd /d /c "set JAVA_HOME=%USERPROFILE%\scoop\apps\microsoft17-jdk\current&& set PATH=%JAVA_HOME%\bin;%PATH%&& mvn -Dmaven.test.skip=true package"`
- Command: `. .\target\daytrader-db2-app-env.ps1; $env:JAVA_HOME = "$env:USERPROFILE\scoop\apps\microsoft17-jdk\current"; $env:Path = "$env:JAVA_HOME\bin;" + $env:Path; java -jar target\io.openliberty.sample.daytrader8.war --server.port=19095`
- Command: `Invoke-WebRequest -UseBasicParsing -Method Post -Uri 'http://127.0.0.1:19095/daytrader/app' -ContentType 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded' -Body 'action=login&uid=uid%3A0&passwd=xxx'`
- Command: `Invoke-WebRequest -UseBasicParsing -Uri 'http://127.0.0.1:19095/daytrader/rest/quotes/s:1' -WebSession <authenticated session>`
- Passed: 6
- Failed: 0
- Skipped: 0

## Notes

- The initial anonymous `buildDB` live probe was long-running because it repopulates the full
  DayTrader dataset. Server-side evidence showed the request reached the Spring MVC controller on
  the fresh DB2 lane instead of failing fast with `401`, and the follow-on login/quote probes
  confirmed that the seed path completed successfully.
- The recurring DB2 warning `4474` about read-only enforcement still appears during scheduled reads
  and remains outside this bootstrap fix.

## Follow-up

- Tester can rerun `t20.5` against the same DB2 primary lane to refresh browser and contract
  evidence on top of the restored bootstrap path.
